title: Marie Curie, the first woman to receive a Nobel Prize radium and polonium
content: Marie Skłodowska-Curie, a Polish-French physicist and chemist, was the first woman to receive a Nobel Prize and the only woman to receive two Nobel prizes. While studying uranium’s rays, she discovered new elements and named them polonium and radium. In 1903, Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics for her research of radiation phenomena. She was also the first woman in France to attain a PhD in Physics, and the first woman to teach at the Sorbonne. In 1911, she won a second Nobel Prize in chemistry for the discovery of polonium and radium. Curie’s pioneering work in radiation led to new cancer treatments. title: Marie Curie (1867–1934) was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize ...
content: She shared the 1903 Nobel Prize in Physics with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel and won the 1911 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for isolating radium

title: Marie Curie (1867–1934): Twice Nobel Laureate and Her Enduring ...
content: In 1898, the Curies discovered the elements polonium and radium, a feat that earned them international acclaim and the 1903 Nobel Prize in Physics, shared with Henri Becquerel [[1](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF1)]. Marie Curie's subsequent isolation of pure radium in 1910 further solidified her reputation, culminating in a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, in 1911 [[2](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF2)]. Date:Circa 1895 Source:Wikipedia File: Pierre Curie et Marie Skłodowska Curie, 1895 [[9](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF9)] Author:Unknown author Permission:Public domain. In 1896, Henri Becquerel's discovery of natural radioactivity provided the impetus for Curie's groundbreaking research [[10](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF10)]. These discoveries were monumental, establishing the Curies' reputation in the scientific community and earning them the 1903 Nobel Prize in Physics, shared with Henri Becquerel for their combined work on radioactivity [[13](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF13)]. Her daughter, Irène Joliot-Curie, followed in her footsteps, contributing to the family's scientific legacy by winning a Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1935 [[18](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11390158#REF18)]. [[PMC free article](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2582731/)] [[PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15482656/)] [[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=Yale%20J%20Biol%20Med&title=The%20life%20and%20legacy%20of%20Marie%20Curie&volume=76&publication_year=2003&pages=167-180&pmid=15482656&)]. doi: 10.1080/09553002.2022.2027542 [[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1080/09553002.2022.2027542)] [[PMC free article](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9723808/)] [[PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35030065/)] [[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=Int%20J%20Radiat%20Biol&title=The%20enduring%20legacy%20of%20Marie%20Curie%3A%20impacts%20of%20radium%20in%2021st%20century%20radiological%20and%20medical%20sciences&volume=98&publication_year=2022&pages=267-275&pmid=35030065&doi=10.1080/09553002.2022.2027542&)]. doi: 10.3747/co.2007.110 [[DOI](https://doi.org/10.3747/co.2007.110)] [[PMC free article](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1891197/)] [[PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17576470/)] [[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=Curr%20Oncol&title=Pierre%20Curie%2C%201859-1906&volume=14&publication_year=2007&pages=74-82&pmid=17576470&doi=10.3747/co.2007.110&)]. [[PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/4912149/)] [[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=%20J%20Nucl%20Med&title=Translation%20of%20an%20historic%20paper.%20On%20a%20new%2C%20strongly%20radioactive%20substance%2C%20contained%20in%20pitchblende%3A%20by%20M.P%20Curie%2C%20Mme%20P.%20Curie%20and%20M.G.%20B%C3%A9mont%3B%20presented%20by%20M.%20Becquerel&volume=11&publication_year=1970&pages=269-270&pmid=4912149&)].

title: The Nobel Prize in Physics 1903 - NobelPrize.org
content: ## Nobel Prizes and laureates. ## Nobel Prize in Physics 1903. # Nobel Prize. Photo from the Nobel Foundation archive. The Nobel Prize in Physics 1903 was divided, one half awarded to Antoine Henri Becquerel "in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by his discovery of spontaneous radioactivity", the other half jointly to Pierre Curie and Marie Curie, née Skłodowska "in recognition of the extraordinary services they have rendered by their joint researches on the radiation phenomena discovered by Professor Henri Becquerel". MLA style: The Nobel Prize in Physics 1903. The 14 laureates' work and discoveries range from quantum tunnelling to promoting democratic rights. ### Explore prizes and laureates. #### About the Nobel Prize organisation. ##### The Nobel Foundation. ##### The prize-awarding institutions. For more than a century, these academic institutions have worked independently to select Nobel Prize laureates. Several outreach organisations and activities have been developed to inspire generations and disseminate knowledge about the Nobel Prize.
